key_insight: EKSFT improves low-data supervised fine-tuning by masking tokens with high entropy or high KL divergence, so the model learns task-relevant skills without drifting far from its pre-trained distribution.
engineering_relevance: For LLM post-training pipelines, this offers a practical way to build a better RL warm-start that preserves exploration capacity and can improve downstream reasoning performance.
method: Compute token-level entropy and KL divergence against a reference model.
Mask high-uncertainty or distribution-shifting tokens during SFT.
Fine-tune only on the remaining tokens to selectively imprint task knowledge.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that EKSFT consistently outperforms standard SFT on mathematical reasoning benchmarks and that RL from EKSFT yields better post-RL performance.
watch_item: The masking strategy depends on a suitable reference model and thresholding scheme, which may require tuning to avoid removing too much useful supervision.
paper_title: Entropy-KL Divergence-based Token Masking: A Novel Approach for Selective Fine-tuning of Large Language Models
